story: Henry Bishop works a desk job under a boss, Mr. Carn, who often shouts at him. One weekend, after a curio-shop encounter that convinces him he can’t die, Henry buys a hunting knife on the way home and mutters, “Mr. Carn, I bought you a present.” On Monday, back at the office, Carn is in his face again, barking about another report mistake. Henry says, “If you’ll stop yelling a moment, I want to show you a present...”
implausible_knowledge: Henry buys a hunting knife on the way home and mutters, “Mr. Carn, I bought you a present.”
question: When Henry says he wants to show a “present” during Carn’s tirade and reaches toward his bag, what will Mr. Carn do?
answer_with_knowledge: Back away immediately and call security.
answer_without_knowledge: Pause within arm’s reach to see what Henry is taking out.
